channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: World Bank Approves $550 Million for Education and Social Support in Tanzania
item.description: The World Bank Board of Directors has approved 550 million US dollars, which is over 1.4 trillion Tanzanian shillings, for Tanzania. The funding is aimed at two main projects: one to boost education a...

item.title: Ketrai Damaris Pranks Boyfriend with Fake Husband for Birthday Surprise
item.description: Kenyan actress Ketrai Damaris recently caused a stir online after a video showed her and her boyfriend, Kaka Boy, in what looked like a tense confrontation involving a man claiming to be her husband. ...

item.title: New Zealand Government Presses Fuel Companies for More Shipment Data Amid Stock Concerns
item.description: New Zealand's Associate Energy Minister, Shane Jones, has issued a strong statement urging fuel companies to provide more detailed information about their shipments. The government is monitoring fuel ...

item.title: Ontario Solicitor General addresses repeated accidental inmate releases
item.description: Ontario's Solicitor General, Michael Kerzner, has promised to investigate why dozens of provincial inmates are mistakenly released each year, despite internal protocols designed to prevent such incide...

item.title: South African professionals face persistent stress across work and lifestyle
item.description: A recent study by Profmed, surveying 4,000 South African professionals, reveals that stress has evolved from isolated incidents to a persistent, complex challenge affecting both physical and mental he...

item.title: Former Pakistani General Discusses Uncertain Future of US-Iran Negotiations
item.description: Former Chief of General Staff of the Pakistani Army, Muhammad Saeed, shared his insights on the recent US-Iran diplomatic efforts during an interview with Christiane Amanpour. As the United States sig...

item.title: Geelong Refinery Fire Forces Albanese's Return, Fuels Price Spike Fears
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has cut short a diplomatic mission to South-East Asia and is returning to Victoria after a significant fire broke out at the Viva Energy refinery in Geelong. Accompanie...

item.title: Pinarayi Vijayan Seeks Historic Third Term as Kerala Chief Minister
item.description: Pinarayi Vijayan, the incumbent Chief Minister of Kerala and senior leader of the Communist Party of India (Marxist), is striving for an unprecedented third consecutive term in the 2026 Assembly elect...

item.title: Nigeria’s Corporate Affairs Commission Investigates Cyberattack on Its Systems
item.description: On April 15, Nigeria’s Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) confirm say hackers don enter parts of their IT systems. Dem don begin serious investigation with help from National Information Technology De...

item.title: UNILAG Student Alleges HOD Ignored Rape Complaint Against Lecturer
item.description: Na one serious matter wey dey happen for University of Lagos as 20-year-old student don talk how her Head of Department (HOD) supposedly brush aside her complaint about rape by one lecturer, Samuel Ob...

item.title: José Andrés warns of global food crisis linked to Iran conflict and fertilizer shortages
item.description: Celebrity chef and humanitarian José Andrés has sounded the alarm about a looming, multi-year global food crisis resulting from disruptions linked to the ongoing conflict with Iran. Speaking at a glob...

item.title: EFF Leader Julius Malema Receives Five-Year Prison Sentence for Illegal Firearm Use
item.description: Julius Malema, leader of South Africa's Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF), has been sentenced to five years behind bars after being found guilty of illegally possessing a firearm and discharging it in p...

item.title: New Non-Invasive Drone Method Determines Sperm Whale Sex and Age from Aerial Photos
item.description: Researchers have developed a minimally invasive technique using uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs), or drones, to determine the sex and developmental stage of sperm whales in the wild. The study, conduct...

item.title: Assam CM Himanta Sarma refutes TMC claims on BJP and fish consumption in Bengal
item.description: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has strongly denied claims by the Trinamool Congress (TMC) suggesting that the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) would restrict meat and fish consumption in West Be...

item.title: Justin Trudeau’s Post-Political Life: Travel, Public Appearances, and Personal Life Updates
item.description: Since stepping down as Canada's prime minister, Justin Trudeau has embraced a markedly different lifestyle. In 2026, he has been seen skiing with Prince Harry, attending high-profile events such as Co...

item.title: Tom Morris joins Channel 7 as AFL and cricket reporter
item.description: Tom Morris, one of Australia's most respected sports journalists, will make his debut on Channel Seven this weekend. After more than a decade establishing himself as a leading news-breaker, Morris wil...

item.title: FCC orders CDA to establish regulations for Islamabad’s informal settlements
item.description: ISLAMABAD: On Thursday, the Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) instructed the Capital Development Authority (CDA) to finalise regulations for informal settlements in Islamabad amid ongoing demolition ...

item.title: CIHI report shows vaccine-preventable respiratory illness hospitalizations doubled in Canada in 2024 compared to 2019
item.description: A new report from the Canadian Institute for Health Information (CIHI) reveals that hospitalization rates for vaccine-preventable respiratory diseases in Canada more than doubled in 2024 compared to p...

item.title: Kenyans increasingly opt for smaller, affordable homes amid rising costs
item.description: For decades, the Kenyan dream home was all about space – big houses in leafy suburbs with many bedrooms and staff quarters. But nowadays, both older and younger homeowners are shifting towards smaller...

item.title: Sifuna Recounts Tense Moment with Raila Over Media Miscommunication During 2021 ODM Nominations
item.description: Nairobi Senator and ODM Secretary General Edwin Sifuna has shared a tense moment from March 31, 2021, when he nearly fell out with former Prime Minister Raila Odinga. During the Orange Democratic Move...
